document:
DIGIT Studio Design Architecture Overview

DIGIT Studio is a comprehensive e-governance platform developed by eGOV Foundation that enables the design, deployment, and management of public services. The architecture follows a microservices pattern with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers.

The platform consists of three main application entry points that communicate through an API Gateway to access various backend services organised into three categories:
- Studio Services: Services specific to DIGIT Studio functionality
- Shared Services: Reusable services across multiple modules
- Core Platform Services: Foundational infrastructure services
- Dashboard/Transformer: Specialised analytics and transformation services

DIGIT Studio Presentation Layer — Top-Level Applications

These are the user-facing applications that serve as entry points to the platform:

1. Studio Designer: Visual interface for designing and configuring public services, forms, and workflows. This is where program managers and administrators build services without writing code.

2. Studio Service Application: End-user application for accessing and consuming configured public services. This is what citizens and employees use to submit applications and track service requests.

3. Studio Service Dashboard: Analytics and monitoring dashboard for tracking service performance and usage metrics. Used by administrators to monitor service adoption and performance.

DIGIT Studio API Gateway

The API Gateway acts as the single entry point for all client requests from the three front-end applications (Studio Designer, Studio Service Application, Studio Service Dashboard).

The API Gateway handles:
- Request routing to appropriate microservices
- Authentication token validation
- Rate limiting and throttling
- Request/response transformation
- Load balancing across service instances

All communication between front-end applications and backend services goes through the API Gateway.

DIGIT Studio — Studio Services

These services are specific to DIGIT Studio functionality:

- Service Config/Template: Manages service configuration templates and schemas for creating new public services
- Public Service Init: Handles initialisation and bootstrapping of new public service instances
- Public Service: Core service for managing public service lifecycle and operations
- Studio PDF: Generates PDF documents for applications, certificates, and reports
- Boundary Management: Manages geographical and administrative boundaries (districts, wards, zones)
- Service Requests: Handles citizen service requests, tracking, and status management
- Transformer: Data transformation service for format conversion and data mapping between systems

DIGIT Studio — Shared Services

Reusable services that support multiple modules across the platform:

- PDF Service: Generic PDF generation and manipulation service
- Boundary Service: Core boundary data service for geographical hierarchies
- Tenants: Multi-tenancy management for different government bodies/departments
- Report Service: Configurable reporting engine for generating various reports
- Queue Service: Message queue management for asynchronous processing
- Workflow: Business process and workflow orchestration engine
- Dashboard Backend Service: Backend APIs supporting the analytics dashboard

Shared services are reused across multiple DIGIT modules and are not specific to Studio alone.

DIGIT Studio — Core Platform: Authentication & Authorization

Foundational services for identity, access, and security:

5.1 Authentication & Authorization Block:
- User Service: User management, profiles, and credentials
- Individual Service: Citizen/individual identity and profile management
- Access Control: Role-based access control (RBAC) and permissions management
- Encryption: Data encryption/decryption services for sensitive information
- MDMS (Master Data Management Service): Centralised reference data management

5.2 Utility Services Block:
- Inbox: Unified inbox for notifications, tasks, and pending actions
- Localisation: Multi-language support and translation management
- File Store: Document and file storage service

Authentication Flow:
Client Request → API Gateway → User Service (Authenticate) → Access Control (Authorise) → Target Service

DIGIT Studio — Core Platform: Data Pipeline & Notification Services

5.3 Data Pipeline Block:
- Any Service: Generic service interface for write/update operations
- Any Registry: Generic registry for read operations and data lookups
- Persister: Data persistence service that writes to the database
- Signed Audit: Cryptographically signed audit trail for compliance
- Indexer: Data indexing service for search optimisation

5.4 Notification Services Block:
- Billing: Bill generation and billing cycle management
- SMS: SMS notification gateway
- Email: Email notification service
- App Notification: Push notification service for mobile applications

5.5 Financial Services Block:
- Billing Service: Detailed billing operations and calculations
- Collection Registry: Payment collection tracking and registry
- Payment Gateway: Integration with payment processors for online payments
- HRMS: Human Resource Management System for employee data

5.6 Observability Block:
- Telemetry: Application performance monitoring and metrics collection

DIGIT Studio — Data Storage Layer and Data Flow Patterns

Data Storage:
- Relational Database (PostgreSQL): Primary data store for transactional data
- Analytical Database (Elasticsearch): Analytics, full-text search, and log aggregation

Write/Update Data Flow:
Any Service → Queue Service → Persister → PostgreSQL
                            ↓
             Signed Audit → Indexer → Elasticsearch

Read Data Flow:
Any Registry → PostgreSQL (direct read)
Dashboard Backend Service → Elasticsearch (analytics queries)

Authentication Flow:
Client Request → API Gateway → User Service (Authenticate)
                             ↓
              Access Control (Authorise) → Target Service

DIGIT Studio — Key Design Principles

1. Microservices Architecture: Each service has a single responsibility and can be deployed independently.

2. Event-Driven Processing: Write operations go through a queue for reliable async processing.

3. Audit Compliance: All data changes are cryptographically signed for audit trails.

4. Multi-Tenancy: Platform supports multiple government bodies with isolated data.

5. Dual Database Strategy: PostgreSQL for ACID transactions, Elasticsearch for analytics.

6. API-First Design: All services communicate through well-defined APIs via the gateway.

Integration Points:
- Payment Processing: Payment Gateway, Collection Registry, Billing Service — handles online payments
- Notifications: SMS, Email, App Notification — multi-channel citizen communication
- Document Generation: Studio PDF, PDF Service, File Store — generate and store documents
- Workflow Automation: Workflow, Queue Service, Inbox — automate approval processes

DIGIT Studio — Technology Stack

Layer → Technology:
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Search/Analytics: Elasticsearch
- Message Queue: Kafka (typical for DIGIT)
- API Gateway: Zuul/Spring Gateway (typical for DIGIT)
- Container Orchestration: Kubernetes

Data is stored in PostgreSQL as the primary transactional store, and indexed in Elasticsearch for analytics and full-text search. Services communicate asynchronously via Kafka message queues for write/update operations. Kubernetes manages container deployment and scaling.

DIGIT Studio — Public Service API: Service Init APIs

The Generic Public Service API enables the creation and management of configurable public services using DIGIT 2.9 core services. It provides a unified approach to handle service registration, application submission, workflow management, and record generation.

Service Init APIs are used to register and manage public services.

1.1 Search Services
GET /public-service-init/v1/service
Search for registered services by various criteria.
Headers: X-Tenant-Id (Required) — Tenant identifier (e.g., st, dev)
Query Parameters:
- businessService (string, optional): Filter by business service (e.g., newtl, PGR)
- module (string, optional): Filter by module (e.g., tradelicence)
- serviceCode (string, optional): Filter by unique service code
Response: ServiceInitResponse

1.2 Create Service
POST /public-service-init/v1/service
Register a new public service.
Headers: X-Tenant-Id (Required)
Request Body: ServiceInitRequest
Example: tenantId, businessService (e.g. PGR), module (e.g. Complaint_Management_System), status (ACTIVE)
Response: ServiceInitResponse (HTTP 202)

1.3 Update Service
PUT /public-service-init/v1/service/{serviceCode}
Update an existing service configuration.
Path Parameter: serviceCode (unique service code, e.g., complaint_management_system-pgr-svc-2025-11-25-008445)
Request Body: ServiceInitRequest
Response: ServiceInitResponse (HTTP 202)

DIGIT Studio — Public Service API: Application APIs

Application APIs handle the lifecycle of service applications submitted by citizens.

2.1 Search Applications
GET /public-service/v1/application/{serviceCode}
Search applications for a specific service.
Headers: X-Tenant-Id (Required)
Path Parameters: serviceCode (unique service code)
Query Parameters:
- ids (array): Filter by application IDs
- businessService (string): Filter by business service
- module (string): Filter by module
- applicationNumber (string): Filter by application number
- status (string): Filter by status (INACTIVE, ACTIVE, INWORKFLOW)
- boundaryCode (string): Filter by boundary code
- createdBy (string): Filter by creator UUID
- mobileNumber (string): Filter by applicant mobile number
Response: ApplicationResponse

2.2 Create Application
POST /public-service/v1/application/{serviceCode}
Create a new application for a service.
Headers: X-Tenant-Id (Required)
Path Parameters: serviceCode
Request Body: ApplicationRequest — includes tenantId, module, businessService, status, channel, serviceDetails, applicants, address, documents, and workflow action (e.g. SAVE_AS_DRAFT)
Response: ApplicationResponse (HTTP 202)

2.3 Update Application
PUT /public-service/v1/application/{serviceCode}
Update an existing application (including workflow actions such as VERIFY_AND_FORWARD, APPROVE, REJECT).
Request Body: ApplicationRequest with application id included
Response: ApplicationResponse (HTTP 202)

DIGIT Studio — Public Service API: Record APIs

Record APIs manage approved applications and issued licenses/certificates.

3.1 Search Records
GET /public-service/v1/record/_search
Search for records (approved applications/licenses).
Headers: X-Tenant-Id (Required) — Tenant identifier
Query Parameters:
- ids (array): Filter by record IDs
- recordNumber (string): Filter by record number
- applicationNumber (string): Filter by application number
- serviceCode (string): Filter by service code
- businessService (string): Filter by business service
- module (string): Filter by module
- status (string): Filter by status
- includeHistory (boolean): Include historical versions (default: false)
- limit (integer): Number of records to fetch
- offset (integer): Pagination offset
Response: RecordResponse

DIGIT Studio — Service Data Model

The Service object represents a registered public service configuration:

Field | Type | Required | Description
- id: string, read-only — Unique identifier
- tenantId: string, required — Tenant identifier
- businessService: string, required — Business service identifier (e.g., PGR, NewTL)
- module: string, required — Module name (e.g., Complaint_Management_System)
- serviceCode: string, read-only — Auto-generated unique service code
- version: integer, read-only — Configuration version number
- status: enum, optional — INACTIVE or ACTIVE
- additionalDetails: object, optional — Additional metadata
- auditDetails: object, read-only — Audit information

Application statuses: INACTIVE, ACTIVE, INWORKFLOW
Workflow actions: SAVE_AS_DRAFT, SUBMIT, VERIFY_AND_FORWARD, APPROVE, REJECT

Introducing DIGIT Service Delivery Platform

DIGIT Service Delivery Platform is a critical step forward in simplifying and accelerating urban governance application development on the DIGIT 2.9 LTS platform. It introduces a unified, reusable, and configurable architecture to address long-standing issues with fragmented service development.

About DIGIT Service Delivery Platform:
Urban governance projects typically require numerous services and user interfaces (UIs), which are often developed independently, even for similar use cases. This siloed approach leads to:
- Inconsistent implementations across modules
- Duplication of components, increasing technical debt and maintenance costs
- Low reusability, forcing teams to rebuild similar capabilities repeatedly

This fragmentation hinders scalability, slows new rollouts, and inflates maintenance efforts.

DIGIT Service Delivery Platform is a configuration-driven, low-code platform layer on top of DIGIT 2.9 LTS, designed to:
- Standardise development practices
- Accelerate application rollout across urban services
- Centralise and simplify configuration for faster adaptation

It achieves this by providing shared templates, reusable components, and schema-based configuration models that can be applied across different urban governance domains.
